alt_text: A staircase-shaped solid made of identical small cubes, 4 cubes wide. It has three layers: the bottom layer is 4 cubes wide by 3 cubes deep (12 cubes), the middle layer is 4 by 2 (8 cubes) aligned with the back, and the top layer is 4 by 1 (4 cubes) at the back, forming three steps. Diagram not to scale.

An L-shaped garden: a 12 m by 9 m rectangle with its top-right corner cut away. Labelled sides: bottom 12 m, left 9 m, top 7 m, right 4 m. The two unlabelled inner-corner sides work out as 12 - 7 = 5 m (horizontal) and 9 - 4 = 5 m (vertical). Diagram not to scale.

An L-shaped piece of plywood: a 90 cm by 60 cm rectangle with its top-right corner cut away. Labelled sides: bottom edge 90 cm, left edge 60 cm, top edge 50 cm, right edge 35 cm. The two unlabelled cut-out sides are the inner horizontal (90 - 50 = 40 cm) and the inner vertical (60 - 35 = 25 cm). Diagram not to scale.

Line graph of temperature during one day, read every two hours: 6 am 11°C, 8 am 13°C, 10 am 18°C, 12 noon 22°C, 2 pm 24°C, 4 pm 20°C, 6 pm 16°C. The vertical axis shows temperature from 0 to 30 degrees Celsius with gridlines every 5.

Column graph of monthly rainfall at Warrigal Creek Public School in millimetres: January 90, February 70, March 110, April 40, May 60, June 30. The vertical axis shows rainfall in mm from 0 to 120 with gridlines every 20.

A coordinate grid numbered 0 to 10 on both axes. A small arrow shape sits on the left, with its pointed tip marked at the point (3, 2). A bold dashed vertical line runs up the grid at x = 5, labelled as the mirror line. The shape is to be reflected across this line.

A pie chart titled 'Favourite Sport' divided into four sectors. Soccer fills one half (1/2) of the circle, Basketball fills one quarter (1/4), Netball fills one eighth (1/8) and Cricket fills one eighth (1/8). The four fractions add to one whole.

A pie chart titled 'How We Travel to School (120 students)' divided into four sectors. Bus is one third (1/3), Walk is one quarter (1/4), Bike is one quarter (1/4) and Car is one sixth (1/6). These four fractions add to one whole. The chart total is 120 students.

A coordinate grid numbered 0 to 8 on both axes. A right-angled triangle has corners labelled P at (1, 1), Q at (1, 4) and R at (3, 1). The right angle is at P. A dashed arrow points up and to the right, indicating the triangle will be slid 4 units right and 2 units up.
A triangle with a dot inside.
A triangle with a smaller triangle nested inside (correct).
Two separate triangles side by side.
A triangle with a circle inside.
Analogy: a square becomes a square with a smaller square nested inside; a triangle becomes a question box.
Down arrow with a black circle.
Right arrow with a white circle.
Down arrow with a white circle (correct).
Up arrow with a white circle.
Sequence: an arrow (rotating 90° clockwise) beside a circle (alternating black then white). Left arrow + black circle, up arrow + white circle, right arrow + black circle, then a question box.
Symbols: plus, dot, star, dot.
Symbols: dot, plus, star, dot (correct).
Symbols: dot, star, plus, dot.
Symbols: dot, plus, star, star.
Key: circle=plus, square=star, triangle=dot. Message row of shapes: triangle, circle, square, triangle.
A straight row of three cubes.
An L-shaped base of cubes, all one cube high (correct).
A row of cubes with one stacked two high.
A two-by-two square base of cubes.
Reference views: a top view shaped like an L of unit squares (a row of three with one square above the left end) and a front view of three equal squares in a row.
Flag rotated 90 degrees clockwise: pole horizontal pointing right (correct).
Flag rotated 90 degrees anticlockwise: pole points left.
Flag mirror image, not a rotation.
Flag rotated 180 degrees: points downward.
An upright flagpole with a right-pointing V-notched pennant at the top, to be rotated 90 degrees clockwise.
Unfolded square with a single hole near the top-left.
Unfolded square with two holes near the top, mirrored across the vertical centre (correct).
Unfolded square with two holes stacked vertically on the left.
Unfolded square with two holes at the bottom corners.
A square sheet, then folded left-over-right into a tall rectangle with a crease, then the folded sheet with a hole punched near its top-left corner.
A single small upward triangle, black outline, no fill.
A single large square, black outline, no fill.
A single large upward triangle, black outline, no fill.
A single large circle, black outline, no fill.

A three-by-three grid of shapes. Each row runs circle, square, triangle from left to right; each column grows small, medium, large from top to bottom. The bottom-right cell is a dashed question-mark box. The missing shape is a large triangle.
The letter F unchanged.
A backwards F: reflected left-to-right, arms point left, top still at top (correct).
The letter F flipped top-to-bottom.
The letter F rotated 180 degrees.
A block capital letter F beside a dashed vertical mirror line on its right.
A single outlined square containing 4 small black dots, one in each corner.
A single outlined square containing 6 small black dots.
A single outlined square containing 5 small black dots: one in each corner and one in the centre.
A single outlined square containing 3 small black dots.
A sequence of four squares, each holding one more dot than the last: 1 dot, 2 dots, 3 dots, then 4 dots, followed by a question-mark box. The next square in the pattern holds 5 dots.
Two identical right-angled triangles whose long edges can join into a square (correct).
A rectangle and a small square that leave a gap.
An L-shaped piece and a square that would overlap.
Two equal rectangles that make a long rectangle.
A target: one complete square to be made from two of the puzzle pieces.
